questions.text: Which is a collective noun?
questions.explanation: 'Team' names a group, so it is a collective noun.

questions.text: Choose the proper noun.
questions.explanation: 'Delhi' is the name of a particular city, so it is a proper noun.

questions.text: A group of stars is called a ____.
questions.explanation: A group of stars is a constellation.

questions.text: Proper nouns begin with a capital letter.
questions.explanation: Proper nouns (names) always start with a capital letter. True.

questions.text: Choose the correct verb: 'She ____ a song.'
questions.explanation: With 'She' we use 'sings' in the present tense.

questions.text: Past tense of 'eat' is ____.
questions.explanation: The past tense of 'eat' is 'ate'.

questions.text: Past tense of 'go' is 'goed'.
questions.explanation: Past tense of 'go' is 'went', not 'goed'. False.

questions.text: Pick the adjective: 'a delicious cake'.
questions.explanation: 'Delicious' describes the cake, so it is an adjective.

questions.text: The snail moves very ____.
questions.explanation: A snail moves slowly. (Here 'slowly' describes the action.)

questions.text: 'Three' can be used as an adjective in 'three apples'.
questions.explanation: Number words like 'three' describe nouns (how many), so they act as adjectives. True.

questions.text: Replace 'the boys' with a pronoun.
questions.explanation: For more than one (the boys) we use 'they'.

questions.text: This pen is mine. Give it to ____.
questions.explanation: After 'to' we use 'me'.

questions.text: 'It' is used for things and animals.
questions.explanation: 'It' is used for things and animals. True.

questions.text: Which sentence is in the past tense?
questions.explanation: 'I ate rice' is in the past tense.

questions.text: Tomorrow she ____ visit the zoo. (will)
questions.explanation: For the future we use 'will' — she will visit.

questions.text: 'I am reading' is in the present continuous tense.
questions.explanation: 'am reading' shows an action happening now (present continuous). True.

questions.text: Opposite of 'brave' is ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of brave is afraid (cowardly).

questions.text: Opposite of 'begin' is ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of begin is end.

questions.text: Opposite of 'wild' is ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of wild is tame.

questions.text: Opposite of 'ancient' is 'modern'.
questions.explanation: Ancient (old) and modern (new) are opposites. True.

questions.text: A person who teaches students is a ____.
questions.explanation: A teacher teaches students.

questions.text: A place where we borrow and read books is a ____.
questions.explanation: A library is where we read and borrow books.

questions.text: A baby cow is called a ____.
questions.explanation: A baby cow is a calf.

questions.text: A person who flies an aeroplane is called a pilot.
questions.explanation: A pilot flies an aeroplane. True.

questions.text: Fill the blank: 'He is ____ honest man.'
questions.explanation: 'Honest' starts with a vowel sound (the 'h' is silent), so we use 'an'.

questions.text: The cat jumped ____ the wall (across to the other side).
questions.explanation: Jumping across the top means 'over' the wall.

questions.text: We use 'the' before the names of rivers, like 'the Ganga'.
questions.explanation: Rivers take 'the' — the Ganga, the Yamuna. True.

questions.text: Which sentence gives a command?
questions.explanation: 'Close the door.' is an order/command (imperative sentence).

questions.text: An exclamation sentence ends with a ____ mark.
questions.explanation: Exclamations end with an exclamation mark (!).

questions.text: 'Wow! What a beautiful garden!' is an exclamatory sentence.
questions.explanation: It shows strong feeling and ends with '!', so it is exclamatory. True.

questions.text: Which word means the same as 'big'?
questions.explanation: 'Large' means the same as 'big' (synonym).

questions.text: The doctor works in a ____.
questions.explanation: A doctor works in a hospital.
